commit.message:
fix: revert to amd64 build, always apply full deployment.yaml

Reverting ARM64 changes in preparation for switching AKS to amd64 nodes.
Also fixed critical bug: workflow was only updating the image via
'kubectl set image' but never applying deployment.yaml changes
(port 8080, health probes). Now always applies full deployment.yaml.

Changes:
- Dockerfile: Remove all platform-specific flags, back to defaults
- Workflow: Remove QEMU and buildx (not needed for amd64)
- Workflow: Always 'kubectl apply' the full deployment.yaml
- This ensures port, probe, and resource changes reach the cluster

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: add expected failure support to node_compat test harness (#32451)

## Summary

Extends the node_compat test harness to support **expected failures** —
tests that are known to fail with a specific exit code and/or output
pattern. This avoids disabling tests entirely when we know they fail in
a predictable way.

### Changes

- **`PlatformExpectation` enum** — Platform fields (`windows`, `darwin`,
`linux`) now accept `false` (skip), `true` (expect pass), or `{
"exitCode": N, "output": "pattern" }` (expected failure)
- **`ExpectedFailure` struct** — Describes expected exit code and/or
output pattern with `[WILDCARD]` support
- **`handle_expected_failure()`** — Reports pass when failure matches
expectations, fails when test unexpectedly passes or fails differently
- **`tests/node_compat/schema.json`** — JSON Schema for `config.jsonc`
enabling editor validation and autocompletion
- **Unit tests** — Comprehensive deserialization and logic tests for all
config patterns

### Config examples

```jsonc
// Skip on a platform
"test.js": { "windows": false, "reason": "not supported" }

// Expected failure on one platform
"test.js": { "windows": { "exitCode": 1, "output": "error [WILDCARD]" } }

// Expected failure on all platforms
"test.js": { "exitCode": 1, "output": "error [WILDCARD]" }

// Mixed: passes on linux/darwin, expected failure on windows
"test.js": {
  "linux": true,
  "darwin": true,
  "windows": { "exitCode": 1, "output": "Failed [WILDCARD]" }
}
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: bartlomieju <13602871+bartlomieju@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Bartek Iwańczuk <biwanczuk@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remove Windows 7 support code from System.Net.Security (#124555)

## Description

Windows 7 is no longer a supported OS. Remove Win7-specific code paths,
dead branches, and outdated comments from System.Net.Security.

### Product code
- **`SslCertificateTrust.cs`**: Replace `IsWindowsVersionAtLeast(6, 2)`
guard with `IsWindows()` — the version check only excluded Win7, which
is no longer reachable. Remove unused `System.Runtime.Versioning` using.
- **`SslAuthenticationOptions.cs`**: Remove comment about SSL2+TLS1.2
incompatibility on pre-Win10 (code stays, it's still correct).
- **`ExtendedProtectionPolicy.cs`**: Update comment to clarify
ExtendedProtection is supported on all Windows versions supported by
current .NET version.
- **`SslStreamPal.Windows.cs`**: Clarify legacy `SCHANNEL_CRED` comment
— it's for older Windows versions, not specific to Win7.

### Test code
- **`TestConfiguration.cs`**: Remove dead
`!PlatformDetection.IsWindows10OrLater` branch in null encryption
detection.
- **`SslStreamSystemDefaultsTest.cs`**: Simplify `IsWindows &&
WindowsVersion >= 10` to `IsWindows`.
- **`SslStreamCredentialCacheTest.cs`**: Update comment referencing Win8
behavioral change from Win7.

Note: The `SCHANNEL_CRED` / `UseNewCryptoApi` dual-path remains — it
serves Win10 builds before 18836 (pre-TLS 1.3), not Win7.

commit.message:
Fix zstd install rules breaking clr.runtime clean build (#125063)

## Summary

Fix CMake build failure when building `clr.runtime` from a clean build
(instead of `clr.native`).

## Problem

When building `clr.runtime` from clean, the install step fails with:

```
CMake Error at _deps/zstd-build/lib/cmake_install.cmake:53 (file):
    file INSTALL cannot find
    ".../_deps/zstd-build/lib/zstd_static.lib"
```

This happens because zstd's own `install()` rules are included in the
build's install step. When the `runtime` component install runs, it
tries to install `zstd_static.lib` — but that file was never built
because `libzstd_static` is only built as a dependency of
`System.IO.Compression.Native`, which is not part of the `runtime` build
target.

## Fix

Replace `FetchContent` with a direct `add_subdirectory()` call using
`EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL`, which reliably prevents zstd's own `install()` rules
from being included in the component install. The `EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL`
parameter on `add_subdirectory()` has properly suppressed install rules
since CMake 3.14, making it compatible with the project's CMake 3.26
minimum.

The explicit `install()` calls in
`System.IO.Compression.Native/CMakeLists.txt` that install
`libzstd_static` to the correct destinations continue to work as before
since they target the `libzstd_static` target directly (not via zstd's
own install rules).

Fixes #124234

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: runtime
repository.description: .NET is a cross-platform runtime for cloud, mobile, desktop, and IoT apps.

commit.message:
refactor: centralise path utilities into src/path_utils.rs

## Problem

`lexical_normalize` was duplicated verbatim in `src/store.rs` and
`src/commands/decrypt.rs`, making divergence easy to introduce.
`make_repo_relative` was private to `decrypt.rs` but contained the same
logic as the inline stripping in `store::resolve_store_path`.

Additionally, all three uses shared a latent Windows CI bug: on GitHub
Actions the Windows runner's `TEMP` env var uses the 8.3 short name
(`RUNNER~1`) while `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` returns the long name
(`runneradmin`).  Purely lexical normalisation cannot reconcile these
two forms, so `strip_prefix` always failed on Windows, causing every test
that calls `compute_store_path` or `make_repo_relative` to error with
"source file '…' is outside the repository root".

## Changes

### New: `src/path_utils.rs`

Single source of truth for the three path helpers:

- `lexical_normalize` — resolves `.`/`..` without filesystem access (output
  path construction).
- `normalize_for_comparison` — calls `dunce::canonicalize` when the path
  (or its parent) exists on disk; falls back to lexical normalisation.
  Resolves short names and the `\\?\` UNC prefix on Windows.
- `make_repo_relative` — converts an absolute path to repo-relative using
  `normalize_for_comparison` for the strip-prefix check.

`dunce` (v1.0.5) added as an explicit dependency (was already a transitive
dependency).

### `src/store.rs`

- Removed local `lexical_normalize`.
- Switched `compute_store_path` prefix-check to `normalize_for_comparison`
  (fixes the Windows short-name issue).
- `resolve_store_path` now delegates to `make_repo_relative` instead of
  duplicating the inline strip.

### `src/commands/decrypt.rs`

- Removed local `lexical_normalize` and `make_repo_relative`.
- Imports `crate::path_utils::make_repo_relative`.
- Removed the unit tests that tested the now-deleted local copies; the
  equivalent tests live in `path_utils::tests`.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: chat windows no longer grow infinitely — bounded layout + flex scroll

Root cause: .layout had min-height:100vh (unbounded) so .content and
.main-area had no height ceiling. flex:1 + overflow-y:auto on message
containers was useless — parent chain had no upper bound.

Fixes:
- main.css: .layout → height:100vh + overflow:hidden (was min-height:100vh)
  .content: add min-height:0 so grid child respects parent constraint
  .main-area already has flex:1 + overflow-y:auto → normal pages still scroll
- ideation.css: .idea-chat → add flex:1 + min-height:0 so it fills bounded
  parent instead of growing. .idea-messages already has overflow-y:auto ✅
- mkt_ideation.css: same fix for .mkt-chat (used by group_ideation too)

All chat pages (Jarvis /cto, agent /chat, conversation, ideation,
group_ideation) now scroll within their message container instead of
stretching the window.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent contradictory BUY/SELL signals

Two issues fixed:
1. SELL signal no longer fires when price is below buy price — recommending
   a sell at a loss makes no sense. A bearish MA crossover below buy price
   is now suppressed (logged only).
2. BUY dip signal is suppressed when MA trend is bearish (MA15 < MA60).
   Buying into a downtrend compounds losses. The user sees a clear warning
   instead: 'Dip detected but trend is bearish — wait for stabilisation.'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Aggressive cache busting with query strings

- Added timestamp query string to all assets in index.html
- Strips query string from file path lookup to prevent 404s
- Ensures browser never uses cached CSS/JS from older versions
- Solves blank page issue from persistent browser cache
- Every page load gets fresh assets with new timestamp

HTTP headers also strengthened:
- Added Pragma: no-cache and Expires: 0 for index.html
- Maintains 1h cache for static assets (content-addressed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace HTML template with modern Tailwind CSS SaaS landing page + add /privacy route

- New landing page: sticky navbar, hero (gradient), problem/solution/features/
  users/mission/about/responsible-AI sections, and dark footer
- Tailwind CSS via CDN added; existing .tabs/.card/.btn/.output/.spinner/
  .char-counter/.std-desc/.state-selector-bar CSS preserved in <style> block
- All 14 tool panels (lesson, feedback, diff, policy, email, integrity,
  assessment_prep, parent, unit, iep504, quiz, rubric, refine, sitefb)
  preserved with exact element IDs
- All JavaScript preserved exactly: STATE_CONFIG, updateStandards,
  showStdDesc, onStateChange, show, cp, focusMode, call, DOMContentLoaded
- stateSelect dropdown with all 50 states + worldwide option preserved
- {{ state_config_json | safe }} Jinja2 expression preserved
- PRIVACY_HTML variable + @app.route('/privacy') added before __main__
- Privacy page includes full policy text (FERPA, COPPA, data, terms)
  with consistent Tailwind navbar/footer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: change container port from 80 to 8080

.NET 10 defaults to listening on port 8080. The Kubernetes deployment
was configured for port 80, causing health probes to fail and Kubernetes
to repeatedly restart the pod.

Changes:
- containerPort: 80 -> 8080
- livenessProbe port: 80 -> 8080
- readinessProbe port: 80 -> 8080
- Service targetPort: 80 -> 8080 (external port stays 80)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Harden financial precision, fix transaction semantics, and batch log cleanup (#46)

* Initial plan

* Harden ParametricInsurance oracle: fix transaction race, counter cache, and float precision

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Harden financial precision, fix transaction semantics, and batch cleanup

- parametric_insurance.rb: add require 'bigdecimal' (Ruby 3.4+ stdlib removal safety)
- blockchain_minting_service.rb: fix to_wei float precision loss — BigDecimal(amount.to_s) * 10**18
- naas_contract.rb: use cluster.active_trees_count counter cache; use Rational(1,5) instead of Float 0.20
- insurance_payout_worker.rb: replace return with next inside transaction block (semantic trap fix)
- insight_generator_service.rb: replace delete_all with in_batches(of: 10_000) to avoid table lock
- maintenance_record.rb / maintenance/form.rb: fix trailing empty lines (rubocop -A)
- naas_contract_spec.rb: add cluster.reload before check_cluster_health! in tests that create trees
- parametric_insurance_spec.rb: add cluster.reload before evaluate_daily_health! in tests that create trees

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove unused http_session parameter from ZvukMusicClient

The _http_session attribute was stored but never used — the underlying
zvuk-music ClientAsync handles its own HTTP session, and image fetching
goes through self.mass.http_session directly in the provider. Remove the
parameter, attribute, and the now-unused aiohttp import.

commit.message:
Fix leaderboard delete: sync admin status on login + verify deletions

Root cause: Supabase RLS checks is_admin in the DB users table, but the
user's is_admin was false (created before admin check was added). The
delete call silently returned 0 rows with no error.

Fixes:
- loginUser() now syncs admin status for hardcoded admin emails on login
- deleteCompletion() and deleteUser() now verify rows were actually deleted
  and throw descriptive errors if RLS blocks the operation
- Added missing completions UPDATE RLS policy to supabase-setup.sql

After deploying, log out and log back in to sync admin status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: settings LLM providers crash when key_env is None (local-mlx, ollama)

_builtin_providers() called os.environ.get(None) for providers with
key_env=None (local-mlx, ollama) → TypeError → 500 Internal Server Error
→ JSON parse failure in settings page.

Fix: use key_env or '' + check no_auth flag so local providers show as
'available' rather than 'no key'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Blender export script for .fskel skeleton format

Python addon for Blender 3.6+/4.x that exports armature bone hierarchy,
rest transforms, all 20 Blender-compatible pose constraints, and inverse
bind matrices to the binary .fskel format.

Install via Edit > Preferences > Add-ons > Install from Disk.
Appears under File > Export > Talarium Skeleton (.fskel).

Handles: IK, Spline IK, Child Of, Copy Transforms/Rotation/Location/Scale,
Damped Track, Track To, Locked Track, Limit Rotation/Location/Scale,
Transformation, Action, Clamp To, Floor, Maintain Volume, Shrinkwrap, Pivot.

Verified: Python-written files round-trip through the C fskel_load() reader.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
kernel: enable BPF_SYSCALL and CGROUP_BPF for Docker cgroup v2

Docker/runc uses bpf_prog_query(BPF_CGROUP_DEVICE) to manage device
access under cgroup v2. Without CONFIG_BPF_SYSCALL=y the syscall is not
compiled in and the call returns ENOSYS, causing every container start
to fail with:

  error setting cgroup config for procHooks process:
  bpf_prog_query(BPF_CGROUP_DEVICE) failed: function not implemented

The base kernel has CONFIG_BPF=y but CONFIG_BPF_SYSCALL was explicitly
disabled. Enable it along with CONFIG_CGROUP_BPF which gates the cgroup
device-filtering program type.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: auto-apply SDK release changes to docs-vnext

Wire the Label-Ops Documentation Fixer to also trigger on the
sdk-update label. When the SDK Release Monitor creates an issue with
breaking changes and recommended actions, the docs fixer now
autonomously reads the issue, applies renames/updates across
docs-vnext/, and opens a PR.

Adds SDK-specific instructions: class renames, method signature
changes, version bumps, tracing attribute updates, and subclient
path changes.

commit.message:
Redesign popup window with polished UI

- Slide-in animation from bottom-right with ease-out cubic easing
- Rounded border via Canvas polygon
- Accent stripe at top for visual flair
- Context-aware icon (bell for reminders, dot for PRs)
- Hover effects on all interactive elements (buttons, link, close)
- Label-based buttons for cleaner look (no native button chrome)
- Thin divider between message and link
- Dedicated close (X) button in header
